fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#406)

* fix: ramp arrow icon not matching instruction modifier (#255)

The leaflet-routing-machine helper _leftOrRight, used when converting
on/off ramp steps, was purely binary: anything that didn't contain
'left' was returned as 'Right'. This meant an 'on ramp straight'
maneuver ended up with modifier 'Right', rendering a right-turn arrow
even though the instruction text said 'straight'.

Monkey-patch _leftOrRight on the router instance so that modifiers
containing neither 'left' nor 'right' (e.g. 'straight') are passed
through unchanged. 'straight' then becomes the 'Straight' modifier and
getIconName correctly maps it to the continue (straight) arrow icon.

Also add a dedicated test suite covering all modifier cases including
the straight regression.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fixes #4920. Re-enter RunLoop when End is cancelled by IsRunningChanging (#4921)

* Fix StopRequested not reset when IsRunningChanging cancels a stop

When an IsRunningChanging handler vetoed a stop (e.g. 'Are you sure?'
pattern), End() returned early but Run() also returned - leaving
IsRunning=true, StopRequested=true, and no event loop running, causing
an application freeze.

Fix: wrap the try/finally in ApplicationImpl.Run() in a while(true) loop.
After End() returns, check runnable.IsRunning:
- false -> End succeeded, break (no behaviour change)
- true  -> End was cancelled, reset StopRequested=false and re-enter RunLoop

Also adds regression test Run_WhenStopCancelledByIsRunningChanging_RunLoopResumes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: replace prediction engine with calibrated GBM model

BREAKING: Prediction engine completely overhauled to fix absurd scenarios
(e.g. NVDA predicted at $1000/share for bull scenario).

Root causes fixed:
- HMM with only 252 days of data produced unreliable regime estimates
- No bounds on p5/p95 percentiles → extreme values passed to UI
- √T extrapolation (scalePercentiles) amplified errors exponentially
- Raw 1-year historical mean used as future expected return

New architecture:
- Primary model: Calibrated GBM with Student-t quantiles (ν=5)
  - Closed-form percentiles, no Monte Carlo needed
  - Drift shrinkage: blend historical μ with 8% equity prior (w=dataYears/10)
  - Damped volatility for horizons >2yr (mean-reversion à la Fama & French)
  - Hard sanity bounds: [-80%,+100%]/year, [-95%,+1000%] total
- Secondary: Block bootstrap for short horizons (≤6 months)
- HMM demoted to informational regime display only
- GARCH retired from ensemble (no value beyond ~20 trading days)

Expected results (NVDA ~$140, σ≈50%, 12mo):
- Before: Bear -70%, Bull +150% (or worse)
- After: Bear ~-38%, Bull ~+58%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(layers): ensure NEFLayer init tensors are on CPU

When centers or encoder_kwargs["train_data"] arrive on a GPU device
(e.g. from ConvNEFPipeline.fit on CUDA), make_encoders and the bias
computation would mix GPU and CPU tensors, causing a RuntimeError.

Move all intermediate tensors (encoders, encoder_centers,
selected_centers, all_centers) to CPU during __init__. The registered
buffers are relocated to the target device later via .to(device).

fix: ART board auto-load uses jiraReady flag and surfaces auth failure

The relay-connected check in adminHubLoadArtBoardsByProject used
CRG.relay.jiraWin alone.  jiraWin can be transiently null while the
relay is live and jiraReady is already true (e.g. right after page
load or relay reconnect).  Updated to:

...matching the pattern used by 10+ other places in the codebase.

Also applied the same fix to adminHubSearchArtBoards relay check.

Silent auth failure is now visible: the board dropdown shows
'⚠️ Connect to Jira first' instead of doing nothing, and a
tbxDLog entry is written so the Dev Panel captures whether

commit.message:
feat: Build improvements (#142)

Addresses two recent feedback items:
- zig pollutes the user's .cache/zig directory, which may grow large
- Selene manifests take too long to write

The first item is managed by providing zig with a cache dir, which
subsequently sets `ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR` in the subprocess environment.

The second is managed by providing a `complete_manifest` bool flag to
`build()`. When True, the manifest aims to be a complete picture of the
build stages, for use with sharing and diagnosis. When False (default),
less information is written to the manifest. At current this reduced
information is in the form of stripping input artifact or intermediate
artifacts in the manifest, keeping only the final binary.

* feat: Implement TC Status message and J1939-81 compliance fixes

This commit addresses the TC/VT startup order issue where the Task Controller
would not be discovered by implements when starting after the Virtual Terminal.

Key Changes:

- Add TC Status message (PGN 0xCB00, Command 0x0E) transmitted every 2 seconds
  per ISO 11783-10 B.8.1 for periodic TC announcement to late-joining clients

- Implement identify_task_controller() callback to respond to VT queries
  with TC number display (ISO 11783-10 B.5.4/B.5.5)

- Add J1939-81 section 4.4.4.1 compliance: 250ms delay after address claim
  before transmitting other messages for both TC and TECU

- Add TECU address claim validation with proper error handling and cleanup
  if TECU fails to claim its fixed address (address 128)

- Add timestamped logging (HH:MM:SS.mmm) to all application and library logs
  for better debugging and traceability

- Fix lambda capture and send_can_message API compatibility issues

Fixes:
- TC not discovered when starting after VT
- TECU crashes due to AgIsoStack++ library update requirement
- Identify Task Controller callback

* Apply suggestions from code review

`clang-tidy`: resolve `bugprone-implicit-widening-of-multiplication-result` (#496)

* `clang-tidy`: resolve `bugprone-implicit-widening-of-multiplication-result`

Some tests multiply integer constants or variables in contexts where the

Replace contract factories with registration-based ContractRegistry (#126511)

> [!NOTE]
> This PR was created with the assistance of GitHub Copilot.

## Summary

Replace `IContractFactory<T>` and 26 named factory classes with a flat
registration API: `ContractRegistry.Register<T>(version, creator)`.

## Motivation

The existing factory pattern requires modifying multiple files to add
contract support for new runtimes (e.g., NativeAOT). Each contract has a
dedicated factory class with a hardcoded version switch. Adding a new
runtime means touching every factory.

## Changes

### New registration API
```csharp
// Any package registers its contracts the same way:
public static void Register(ContractRegistry registry)
{
    registry.Register<IThread>(1, static t => new Thread_1(t));
    registry.Register<IRuntimeTypeSystem>(1, static t => new RuntimeTypeSystem_1(t));
    // ...
}
```

### CachingContractRegistry
- Zero contract knowledge — pure `(Type, int) → creator` registry
- Takes `params Action<ContractRegistry>[]` for composable registration
- Built-in CoreCLR and external packages use the same `Register<T>()`
API

### Contract constructors
- Simplified to take only `Target target`
- Each reads its own globals internally (Object_1, GC_1, Thread_1, etc.)

### CoreCLRContracts.cs (new)
- Single file registering all 26 CoreCLR contract versions
- Same pattern external packages would use

### Deleted (26 files)
- All named factory classes (ThreadFactory, ExceptionFactory,
ObjectFactory, etc.)

### Enables
- External contract packages (NativeAOT, Mono) without modifying the
main cDAC reader
- `ContractDescriptorTarget.TryCreate(addr, ...,
[CoreCLRContracts.Register, NativeAOTContracts.Register], out target)`

## Testing
All 1406 existing cDAC tests pass.

commit.message:
feat(conv): add diverse ensemble with per-member stage configs

Add member_stages parameter to ConvNEFEnsemble.  When provided, each
ensemble member uses its own stage configuration (patch size, filter
count, strategy), enabling diverse ensembles where members specialize
at different spatial scales.  This increases ensemble diversity beyond
what different random seeds alone provide.

When member_stages is not provided, all members share the same stages
config (existing behaviour).  Length validation ensures member_stages
matches n_members.

Adds per-member stage support to _run_conv_config in the conv_cifar
benchmark suite.  Adds two diverse ensemble configs:
- 6 members with patch sizes 3/3/5/5/7/7 + gcn + std
- Same with per-patch contrast normalization

Two new tests:
- test_diverse_member_stages: verifies different patch sizes per member
- test_member_stages_length_mismatch: validates error on wrong count

goos_goarch (e.g. darwin_amd64). Falls back to pattern field when no
platform-specific match exists. This handles tools like neovim and
